entries.title: About the Course
entries.content: This course gives students a glimpse into how the ideas that guide our study of geometry were first discovered.

entries.title: Placement & Combining Tips
entries.content: This course is intended to follow Part 1; however, the order may be reversed to facilitate combining students.

rows.label: Story of Geometry: Part 2
rows.scheduleInfo:
Story of Geometry: Part 2
1 time/week 15 min

entries.content:
Permission to print for non-commercial use. See Alveary group use policy to use lessons
in a group context.

LINKS: Click text or scan the QR code in the top corner of the lesson plan pages to view
online resources associated with the lessons.

Responsibility for previewing all links rests with the teacher. All links were checked at the
time of publication; however, websites change frequently and may contain objectionable
content. Please report broken links by contacting us through our website.

blocks.text: Optional: Use a picture, map, or engaging question to prepare the student to receive the idea from the day’s lesson. Talk about one or two unknown words that will be crucial to understanding.
Read the new passage out loud or have the student read it orally or silently.
Have the student narrate orally or in writing.
• Give students an opportunity to share their thoughts on the lesson and any connections made.
Optional: Attempt to recreate some of the problems from the stories.

lessons.title: 15m Story of Geometry: Part 2 - Lesson 1
lessons.body:
The Rules of the Game 
⍞ Materials: String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.10 p.79-81 (UK 72-75) "With this" - "agreements and definitions."

• How was Thales's use of geometry similar to and different from the Egyptians'?

lessons.title: 15m Story of Geometry: Part 2 - Lesson 2
lessons.body:
Circles: Terms, Definitions, & Rules 
⍞ Materials: String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow

➜ RECAP 
What did you read last time?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.10 p.82-84 (UK 75-78) "We can hardly" - "than two angles."

➜ PRACTICE 
Using your geometry tools, draw a line that is 2 inches long. Use this as the radius for a circle. What is the diameter? Using the bottom picture as a model, divide the circle into 6 equal parts.
⍞ Image Resource: Materials: String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow p.83

lessons.title: 15m Story of Geometry: Part 2 - Lesson 3
lessons.body:
Triangles: Terms, Definitions, & Rules
⍞ Materials: String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow

➜ RECAP 
What did you read last time?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.10 p.85-87 (UK 78-80) "Speaking of" - "180 degrees."

➜ PRACTICE
Using your geometry tools, construct a right triangle with a base that is 2 inches long. Construct a similar triangle with a base that is 4 inches long.

lessons.title: 15m Story of Geometry: Part 2 - Lesson 4
lessons.body:
Lines: Terms, Definitions, & Rules
⍞ Materials: String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow

➜ RECAP 
What did you read last time?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.10 p.88-91 (UK 81-84) "Since two" - "Greek theoretical geometry!"

➜ PRACTICE
Look at the picture. What must be the measure of angle 1? [Use what you know about equilateral triangles.] What about angle 2? [Use what you know about right triangles.] How do you know? Can you figure out the measures of the unlabeled angles?
⍞ Image Resource: Materials: String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow p.90

• Draw or paint a picture that shows how Pythagoras determined that the earth was round.

lessons.title: 15m Story of Geometry: Part 2 - Lesson 5
lessons.body:
Pythagoras Studies with Thales
⍞ Materials: String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow, World Wall Map

➜ RECAP 
What did you read last time?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.11 p.93-95 (UK 86-88) "The early story" - "also spherical."

➜ LOCATE 
⍞ Map Resource: World Wall Map
∞ Map Link: Croton
Look at the map and find Croton. Then find it on your wall map.

lessons.title: 15m Story of Geometry: Part 2 - Lesson 7

A Famous Theorem
⍞ Materials: String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow

➜ RECAP 
What did you read last time?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.12 p.98-99 (UK 91-92) "The most famous" - "Secret Brotherhood."

• Copy the tile illustration on p.101. Pretend you are Pythagoras explaining this truth to your new initiates.

⍞ Materials: String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow

lessons.title: 15m Story of Geometry: Part 2 - Lesson 10
lessons.body:
Pythagoras' Theorem Defined
⍞ Materials: String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow

➜ RECAP 
What did you read last time?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.12 p.104-105 (UK 96-97) "Look again!" - "sacrifice renowned!"

➜ VIEW
Video on Pythagorean Theorem
∞ Video Link: Pythagorean Theorem

➜ PRACTICE
Construct your own 3-4-5 right triangle. Use cubes, tiles, or graph paper to prove the Pythagorean Theorem.

lessons.title: 15m Story of Geometry: Part 2 - Lesson 18

Whole Numbers & Geometric Shapes
⍞ Materials: String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow

➜ RECAP 
What did you read last time?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.14 p.118-119 (UK 109-112) "Numbers, they" - "disastrous experiment."

➜ PRACTICE
Look at the pictures. What would be the next square number? What would be the next triangular number? Draw pictures.
⍞ Image Resource: Materials: String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow p.118-119

• IMPORTANT DATES
Hippasus of Metapontum (c.500 B.C.)

terms.content: · String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow: Tell what you know of the Pythagorean Theorem.
· String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow: Tell what you know of the Golden Mean.
· String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow: Tell about some of the geometrical feats that the Greeks discovered.
